title: Create Proper File Structure
prompt:
# TASK 1: Create Proper File Structure

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Electron app with all Three.js code inline in index.html.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 2.4 (File Structure)

## OBJECTIVE
Refactor into separate files: styles.css, renderer.js, simplified index.html.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Create demo/ directory
2. Create src/renderer/styles.css with base dark theme styles
3. Create src/renderer/renderer.js - extract ALL JavaScript from index.html into this ES module
4. Simplify index.html to only: doctype, head with CSS link, body with viewport div and module script import

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- App opens with dark background
- Three.js renders (grid, axes, cube)
- Console shows success message
- No errors

## NEXT TASK
Replace placeholder mouse controls with OrbitControls

title: Add OrbitControls
prompt:
# TASK 2: Add OrbitControls

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** renderer.js has custom spherical/isDragging mouse handling code.
**Read first:** Three.js OrbitControls documentation

## OBJECTIVE
Replace ALL custom mouse handling with Three.js OrbitControls.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Import OrbitControls from Three.js addons/examples
2. DELETE the entire custom mouse handling (spherical object, isDragging, updateCamera function, ALL mouse event listeners)
3. Create OrbitControls with damping enabled
4. Configure: left mouse = rotate, right mouse = pan, scroll = zoom
5. Set camera up vector to (0,0,1) for Z-up orientation
6. Set initial camera position for isometric-like view
7. Call controls.update() in animation loop

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Left drag rotates view around origin
- Right drag pans
- Scroll zooms
- Movement is smooth (damping)
- Z-axis (blue) points UP

## NEXT TASK
Add header bar with View dropdown

title: Add Header with View Dropdown
prompt:
# TASK 3: Add Header with View Dropdown

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full-window viewport with OrbitControls.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.1 (Layout) and 3.2.2 (View Controls)

## OBJECTIVE
Add header bar with app title and view preset dropdown.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Wrap everything in an #app container with flexbox column layout
2. Add #header div (32px height) with:
   - App title 'ClaudeCAD' on left
   - View dropdown on right with options: Isometric (default), Front, Back, Top, Bottom, Left, Right
3. Style header with dark theme colors from spec Appendix B
4. Update renderer.js to size Three.js to #viewport div (not window)
5. Add view preset positions (use spec section 3.2.2 for reference)
6. Handle dropdown change to snap camera to selected preset

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Header visible at top with title and dropdown
- Selecting 'Top' shows view looking straight down at grid
- Selecting 'Front' shows view from Y-axis direction
- Selecting 'Isometric' restores angled view
- OrbitControls still work after changing preset

## NEXT TASK
Add chat panel and status bar

title: Add Chat Panel and Status Bar
prompt:
# TASK 4: Add Chat Panel and Status Bar

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Header with dropdown, viewport below.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md sections 3.1, 3.5, 3.6

## OBJECTIVE
Add chat panel at bottom and status bar at very bottom.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add #chat-panel div after viewport:
   - Message display area (scrollable, empty for now)
   - Input area with textarea and Send button
   - Fixed height ~180px initially
2. Add #status-bar div at very bottom:
   - Left side: status text showing 'Ready'
   - Right side: placeholder for model stats
   - Height 24px
3. Style everything with dark theme colors from Appendix B
4. Use flexbox so viewport takes remaining space
5. Update renderer.js to use ResizeObserver for viewport sizing

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Layout from top to bottom: header, viewport, chat, status bar
- Chat has empty message area and input with Send button
- Status bar shows 'Ready'
- Viewport not squashed or distorted
- All colors match dark theme

## NEXT TASK
Make chat panel resizable

title: Make Chat Panel Resizable
prompt:
# TASK 5: Make Chat Panel Resizable

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Fixed-height chat panel.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.5.1

## OBJECTIVE
Allow user to resize chat panel by dragging its top edge.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add a resize handle div at top of chat panel (4px tall)
2. Style handle: transparent normally, accent color on hover, ns-resize cursor
3. Implement drag-to-resize:
   - On mousedown: start tracking
   - On mousemove: calculate new height
   - On mouseup: stop tracking
4. Constraints: minimum 100px, maximum 60% of window
5. Prevent text selection during drag
6. Viewport should resize automatically as chat resizes (ResizeObserver handles this)

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Cursor changes to resize cursor on chat top edge
- Handle highlights on hover
- Drag up = chat taller, drag down = chat shorter
- Constraints enforced
- Viewport resizes correctly, no distortion

## NEXT TASK
Add loading spinner overlay

title: Add Loading Spinner
prompt:
# TASK 6: Add Loading Spinner

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full layout with resizable chat.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.2.4 (Loading State)

## OBJECTIVE
Add loading spinner overlay on viewport for when AI is generating.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add overlay div inside viewport container (hidden by default)
2. Overlay: semi-transparent dark background, centered spinner
3. Spinner: CSS-only rotating ring animation, accent color
4. Create showLoading() and hideLoading() functions, expose globally
5. When loading shown: update status bar to 'Generating...'
6. When loading hidden: update status bar to 'Ready'
7. Add temporary keyboard shortcut (L key) to toggle for testing

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Press L: spinner appears centered over viewport
- Spinner rotates smoothly
- Scene visible but dimmed behind overlay
- Status bar shows 'Generating...'
- Press L again: spinner hides, status shows 'Ready'
- Overlay doesn't cover header, chat, or status bar

## NEXT TASK
Remove test cube, finalize Phase 1

title: Remove Test Cube and Finalize
prompt:
# TASK 7: Remove Test Cube and Finalize

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full UI complete, but test cube still in scene.

## OBJECTIVE
Remove test geometry, prepare for Phase 2.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Remove test cube code from renderer.js (geometry, material, mesh, scene.add)
2. Keep the lighting (ambient + directional) - needed for future models
3. Add placeholder loadMesh() function exposed globally (logs message, does nothing yet)
4. Add placeholder for currentMesh variable
5. Update console message to 'ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete'

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- NO cube visible - only empty grid and axes
- Grid on XY plane (horizontal)
- Z-axis (blue) points up
- Header with View dropdown works
- Chat panel with input, resizable
- Status bar shows Ready
- L key toggles spinner
- Console: 'ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete'

## PHASE 1 COMPLETE
Ready for Phase 2: Python Backend
